figures.caption: Figure 1: Noise injection demo. A validation sample near the decision boundary is perturbed by Gaussian noise. Colors indicate class labels. The perturbation here is small in magnitude but changes the sample’s nearest neighbors, potentially altering its contribution signal.
Figure 2: Shapley value shift under the noisy validation set.
Figure 3: Case I with one validation sample. Boundary sample vs non-boundary sample.
Figure 4: Case II with two validation samples.
Figure 5: Case II with two validation samples.
Figure 6: Shapley value correction on the Gaussian dataset.
Figure 7: Shapley value correction on the real-world datasets: Phoneme, Planes2D, and Pol.
Figure 8: Shapley value correction on the real-world datasets: CPU, News20, and CreditCard.
Table 1: Real-world datasets used in our experiments.
